admission_info.presenting_complaint: Acute chest pain radiating to left arm, diaphoresis

diagnosis.description: ST elevation (STEMI) myocardial infarction of anterior wall

ecg_findings.findings: ST segment elevation in V1-V4, reciprocal changes in inferior leads, consistent with anterior STEMI

procedure_details.procedure_type: Diagnostic Cardiac Catheterization with Percutaneous Coronary Intervention
procedure_details.sedation: Moderate sedation with fentanyl and midazolam

cardiac_ct.anatomical_notes: Heavily calcified coronaries, suitable target vessels identified

incision.approach: Standard midline incision from sternal notch to xiphoid

transesophageal_echocardiogram.wall_motion_improvement: Significant improvement in anterior wall motion
transesophageal_echocardiogram.graft_flow_assessment: All grafts patent with good flow

event.description: Hypotension with MAP 55 during bypass weaning
event.intervention: Volume resuscitation with 500 mL crystalloid, inotropic support with epinephrine 0.05 mcg/kg/min initiated
Amiodarone 150 mg IV bolus, followed by infusion at 1 mg/min
event.resolution: Converted to sinus rhythm after 15 minutes

handoff_summary.procedure_performed: Three-vessel CABG (LIMA to LAD, SVG to OM, SVG to RCA)
handoff_summary.intraoperative_course: Complex but successful, required inotropic support, now stable

special_instructions.instruction: Monitor for bleeding, chest tube output
Check ACT, PT/PTT, and platelet count in 1 hour

operative_note.summary: The patient was brought to the operating room and placed under general anesthesia. Following median sternotomy, cardiopulmonary bypass was initiated. The LIMA was harvested and anastomosed to the LAD. Two saphenous vein grafts were harvested from the right leg and anastomosed to the OM and RCA. The patient was weaned from bypass with inotropic support. Hemostasis was achieved, chest tubes placed, and sternum closed in standard fashion. The patient was transferred to the CTICU in stable but critical condition.

post_operative_diagnoses.diagnosis: Three-vessel coronary artery disease, status post CABG x 3

medication.weaning_notes: Titrated down and discontinued on POD 1

complication.management: Amiodarone bolus and infusion, rate control achieved

goal.goals: Hemodynamic stability, wean inotropes, maintain euvolemia
Extubation, ambulation, chest tube removal assessment
Transfer to step-down unit, restart oral medications

medication.status: Restarted with lower dose due to renal function

session.resistance_exercises: Seated leg raises, arm curls with 2 lb weights

discharge_planning.outpatient_cardiac_rehab_referral: Phase II cardiac rehabilitation at outpatient center
